segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Complete Integrated Draft - All Acts

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dawn bled into the Deep Hall as the lichen shifted from bruise-blue to silver-green. With the light came the low sound -- felt first in teeth, then in boot-soles, then up through the ribs.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A breath-long pause -- did you imagine it? -- then:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ward-line held under Serana's palm -- chalk geometry and nine-stroke precision, the sigils she'd drawn before sleep still bright enough to matter. They stole some of the bass out of the air, damping the pulse like thick velvet over a drum. Not all of it. Not anymore.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Scrape from the forge corridor. Tap-tap at the sealed mine. A wet drag through floodwater that should have been still for centuries. The mountain answered with another dull knock that belonged to no mortal heart.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin woke to that slow count and the smell of old smoke and cold iron. The scale in his forearm held its coal-glow, the pulse tightened -- not warning, not ease -- like a hand at the small of his back urging him forward.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The familiar ache had settled in overnight to its hateful normal. He flexed carefully. He could manage small work today -- if he paid every time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana kept her hand on the runes. No prayer came. Nothing answered. The chalk held because chalk didn't need permission -- only ratios and repetition and the stubbornness of someone who hadn't stopped yet.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "They've ringed the entrances,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, voice low and steady.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "We don't wait to be tested to failure. We choose our ground."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex was already up, knives in hand, standing where the Illusion alcove threw tricky reflections into the corners.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Fast careful,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "And if that's impossible, just fast."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She glanced at Caelin's arm.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Beacon-boy, if you could stop being a lighthouse -- "

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Working on it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, and because the truth cost less than a lie, added,

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "I can't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik had grandfather's journal spread on the black table, brass goggles riding his brow, big fingers unshakably precise. His beard smelled faintly of beeswax oil and iron dust.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Forge Road's our best way out -- old ore route, still marked sound when Grandfather walked it. Six hours if stone loves us, twelve if it doesn't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen sat cross-legged near the Abjuration alcove where her vine-ink picked up a little of the ward-light, emerald threaded with silver-gold. She lay her palm to the floor and winced -- stone fever, pressure, the fabric of the place pulled too tight.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "This chamber holds for now,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Everything tied to the sanctum's bones is failing. Whatever hunts us is... patient. Learning the ward geometry."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A smear of darkness slid under the rune-line at her feet -- a thin filament of shadow testing the gap between two chiselled glyphs. The ward flared white-hot.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For one knife-bright instant the shadow became a living silhouette of its owner's hand -- tendons, knuckles, too many joints -- then ignited.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It fled screaming without a sound, scuttling back as a scatter of ash that ran like insects, burned to nothing before it reached the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one breathed.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "They're clever,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said softly from the Necromancy alcove, as if admiring a well-played move in someone else's game. Her purple eyes tracked the ward seam a beat longer.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Not clever enough."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: On the edge of the chamber Durgan stood with his back to stone, eyes pale and unblinking. His shadow lay neat at his boots. It twitched once toward the ward-line when the burned ash fled, and he crushed it flat until his jaw clicked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The pressure of holding it down built behind his eyes like fingers pressing into his temples. When he finally released his concentration, a thin warmth trickled from his left nostril. He wiped it with the back of his hand -- checking, confirming. The skin came away with a single red smear.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: First blood, he thought, and tucked his hand away before anyone could see. How many more before I can't stop it anymore?

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Move before they learn a new trick,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said. She pushed to her feet and didn't look back to check the wards. They held or they didn't. Either way, the only answer was forward.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex rolled a shoulder, felt the pull of healing ribs beneath.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Define intact."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Alive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Everything else is optional."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik snapped the journal shut and slung on his pack.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Forge Road starts through the transmutation arch. Watch your steps. The mountain is currently... structurally questionable but functional."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That phrase has never comforted me,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "It wasn't supposed to,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik replied cheerfully.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It's supposed to be true."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They packed to travel-light: water, rope, wedges and braces for Thornik, rations like stone, no spare comforts. The ward-line pulsed off-beat once, a tired strobe. The bass in the room thickened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Go,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They went.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Forge Road fell away steeply, steps cut straight into living rock by hands that valued efficiency. The air turned wet and metallic; the tunnel smelled of wet granite, old slag, and the copper tang of slow rust. Thornik's lantern burned low, the flame cupped, throwing a disciplined cone of light.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He read the stone as he walked -- hairline fractures, load paths, the cross-rib where an ancient brace took more weight than it should. He laid spring-steel wedges into seams with a quiet click, braces kissing the stone to buy them minutes later when seconds might matter.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Don't touch the walls,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "They're listening."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The sound from below grew clearer as the wards' dampening effect faded with distance. No longer muffled. Present. Rhythmic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: An hour in, the mountain shivered. Dust fell in silky threads. Everyone stopped on instinct, breath held until the tremor passed.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Quickly,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, and they did.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They came to a collapse -- a doorway half-buried by a spill of dwarf-cut granite. Thornik knelt.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Option one: explosive. Brings the ceiling to visit. Option two: levers. Time we don't have."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin stepped beside him, put his right hand on the keystone, and called the smallest heat he could bear -- a focused candle on a pinhead, nothing grand. The scale warmed; the ache sharpened like a pulled muscle. Granite ticked and softened to a bead of glass the crowbar could bite.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik levered. Stone slid. Passage opened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin pulled back, breath hissing. The filaments flared angry red, then settled back to baseline coal-glow. He didn't count what he had left. He didn't know. He only knew there was still enough to hurt and not enough to be careless.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "You know,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said as she followed him through,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "most men would have tried a bonfire to impress me."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'm rationing,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Tragic. I had a speech about stamina prepared."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He looked at her.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Save it for someone persuadable."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A narrow twitch at the corner of her mouth -- caught between miffed and entertained.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You're going to be the end of me, Ember-boy."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Get in line,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex called from the far side, one hand pressed to her ribs as if checking they still held.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We're all waiting our turn."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain answered with a deeper thud that rattled loose grit down Caelin's neck.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved on.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A long corridor followed -- clean, quiet, no falls, no scrapes -- only the heartbeat below growing clearer as the wards' bass-trap effect faded with distance. The sound no longer felt distant.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It felt present -- not beneath them specifically, but everywhere at once, resonating through the mountain like a heartbeat felt through pressed palms.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: In the hush, a shadow-dart whispered along the floor ahead of them, a hair-thin ribbon sliding fast.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It struck an old dwarven inlay they barely saw -- a sliver of ward rune hidden in decorative filigree -- and the floor line erupted in a curtain of sheet-fire two hands high.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dart became a coil of screaming soot-snakes that fled backward into the dark.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Good,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Let it keep proving we shouldn't turn around."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Two hours in, they crawled through a chamber where the ceiling had molted down blocks the size of coffins.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Fast careful,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Step where I step. Don't even breathe loudly."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's boot knocked a small stone. It skittered. A support block shifted a fraction, and the chamber groaned like a sleeping giant turning over. They sprinted the last ten feet in a pelting rain of grit and pea-stone. Not a full collapse. A reminder.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the pursuit-scrapes paused. Listening. Counting. Herding.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "They want us deeper,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said without looking back. His voice was flat, factual.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we go deeper on our terms,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana answered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three hours in, Elowen stopped with her hand to the wall. The rootwork in her arms brightened; a single vine-filament along her wrist blackened another shade, flaking an ash petal onto the floor.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The Weave is screaming ahead. A major brace near a junction is under more stress than it can carry. If it goes while we're under it -- "

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "How long?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Minutes. Hours. Or it started while I was speaking."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Faster,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, and winced as her ribs protested the thought.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They went faster.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Four hours in, another choke point. Thornik measured. Levers would work -- eventually. Explosives would kill them. Caelin set his palm to a stone and gave it heat like a match-head. Glass bloomed under his hand; Thornik heaved; the way opened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain threaded Caelin's lattice, not a scream, a serrated sigh. The scale pulsed angry red for three heartbeats, then faded back to coal-glow. Manageable, for now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara fell in beside him again, voice a ribbon of dark amusement.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Refreshing, this restraint. I've been tracking something hot all morning, but I suppose I can exercise patience."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Tracking the path,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her eyes glinted.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Among other things."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Save those speeches too,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, and kept moving.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A laugh. Low. Honest. Then the heartbeat underfoot stole the humor back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Five hours in, Serana held a thin, mobile ward over them to shed small falls and carry the sound of their feet away. It flickered once, a brief stutter that she forced flat. She didn't meet anyone's eyes.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Define 'fine,'"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Not dying,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Optional,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex muttered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's breath rasped under his cheer.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "We're close to the junction chambers,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Three-way split: mines, smelters, caldera to surface. The caldera stair is our goal."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Unless the mountain disagrees,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Stone always gets a vote,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Today I'm asking it nicely."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Six hours in, the tunnel widened without warning.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They swung into a vast chamber where the walls curved like the inside of a bell.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Bridges of black iron spanned the emptiness -- hundreds of them -- some whole, some snapped mid-arch and hanging by cables older than language. Below, molten seams traced geometric rivers through the dark, glowing like veins under translucent skin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's breath fogged his goggles.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "This isn't natural construction. It's metallurgy on a planetary scale."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's voice echoed softly:

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Or worship with better tools."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Chamber of Echoes.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana held her torch close to inscriptions along the nearest bridge rail -- spiral glyphs carved deep and inlaid with gold now blackened by heat. Caelin brushed one with his gloved fingers. It thrummed faintly under the touch, familiar in the way of fever dreams.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Same pattern as the Seal,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik nodded.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then we're standing in its bloodstream."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex looked down into the molten rivers far below.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Let's try not to puncture anything vital."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum shifted mid-sentence -- Thornik's last word echoed a heartbeat later, repeated perfectly but stretched thin, like parchment under strain.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then Caelin's breath followed, an exhale caught and replayed back to him, slightly off-tempo.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They all stopped.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana whispered,

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Echo."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But the word came back not once, not twice, but in every voice in the group -- each one slightly wrong. Serana, Vex, Nyxara, Caelin, Thornik, Durgan, Elowen -- all overlapping, all whispering echo in a chorus that didn't belong to any of them.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The sound faded like a tide withdrawing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one spoke for a full minute after.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved quickly across one of the intact bridges, giving the central darkness wide berth. The air here tasted of coin and ozone. Light from their torches bled into the rock instead of reflecting, as if the stone drank it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway across, Elowen tilted her head.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Do you hear them?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana frowned.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Just echoes."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "No,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen said.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "They're trying to remember what they were saying."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air quivered. A dozen faint voices whispered over one another, too faint to catch -- then aligned, forming a single phrase in Draconic that Caelin understood without translation.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Hold the flame. Guard the law. Bury the cost."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The same three lines, repeating in different cadences, layered until they blurred into music.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale in his arm began to glow faintly through his sleeve -- a bright warm pulse of recognition.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "I've heard this before,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's face tightened.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Prophecy?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Command,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin whispered.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "But it doesn't know who to give it to."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik produced a tuning rod from his pack -- a spare, cruder than the one the mountain had shattered two days ago, but it would serve -- ignoring Vex's muttered warning.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "If it's an acoustic record, we can pull more data."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He struck the rod lightly against stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The resulting tone spiraled outward, repeating itself, climbing octaves until the air vibrated visibly. The chamber answered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A column of sound formed in the air -- dense enough to distort the light -- and within it flickered outlines of figures at work: massive shapes, part dragon, part machine, hammering something that shone like molten sunrise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then the image stuttered and bled away, leaving silence so heavy it felt deliberate.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A fine mist of dust fell from the ceiling, glowing faintly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen looked up in alarm.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Stop. It's -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Too late. The sound collapsed inward, a sonic implosion that slammed everyone to their knees. Ears rang. Torches snuffed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When Caelin's vision cleared, Thornik was bleeding from the nose, staring dazed at his shattered rod.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Note,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he croaked.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Don't do that again."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex kicked the broken metal off the platform, grimacing at the pull in her ribs.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Brilliant deduction."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara stood very still, eyes half-lidded as if listening inward.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "They aren't ghosts,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said finally.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "They're instructions. The mountain replays them when something familiar enters the system. We woke the archive."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana drew a slow breath.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "An archive of what?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara met her gaze.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Every order the gods ever gave their creations. Every one that failed."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed once -- then again, brighter. The heat licked up Caelin's arm, across his collarbone, behind his eyes. Voices flooded in -- thousands, layered, overlapping. Command structures, prayers, screams of obedience and defiance all at once.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For a second, he saw through their memories: dragons kneeling to unseen figures, humans bound by glowing chains, cities forged then unmade by song.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He staggered, gripping the nearest railing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's hand found his shoulder.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Stay here,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said fiercely.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Stay present."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He breathed through it, the images fading like cooling iron.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana caught his other arm, voice sharp.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Caelin!"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'm fine,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he lied. The scale flickered white-hot for an instant -- lie detected -- then settled back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik, pale but upright, whispered,

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "It recognized him. The system thinks he's one of them."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then we move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Before it decides to collect."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crossed the chamber quickly, their footsteps echoing in unnatural sync. Each time the echoes came back, they were half a beat too slow -- as if something behind them was learning to walk.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Near the far archway, the air shimmered. At first it was just heat distortion from below. Then it wasn't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A voice said,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Caelin."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He froze. The sound came from behind -- his own tone, precise down to the hesitation between syllables.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Everyone turned. The path behind them was empty.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not funny,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said automatically, though no one had spoken.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I didn't -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin began, but stopped. His throat had gone dry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The voice came again, softer.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Caelin. Follow."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik muttered a formula under his breath, trying to anchor the acoustics.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Reflected sound... but there's no source."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's smile was faint.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Oh, there's a source. It just borrowed your mouth to speak."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The echo shifted targets.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Elowen."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her own name, spoken like a sigh through leaves.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She staggered.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The forest uses names for roots. This isn't the forest."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana stepped forward.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Stop listening."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's not how ears work,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex snapped, hand moving to a dagger hilt.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then it spoke again -- Serana's voice this time, steady and cold.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Faith bends. It doesn't always break."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's jaw clenched.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's what I said last night."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "It remembers,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It's collecting words that meant something."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik, because curiosity survived even terror, called softly into the dark.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Thornik."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nothing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He exhaled, relieved -- then heard it whispered back three times in a child's voice, each repetition younger than the last.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stumbled back hard enough to knock his goggles askew.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Never again,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he muttered.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Never -- again."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air trembled, repeating the words in exact cadence:

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Never again."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then quieter:

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Never."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Light warped with the next pulse of the hum. The walls sweated condensation that smelled of iron and oil. Their torches flared green, then white. The flame stretched sideways, casting shadows that now mouthed along with the voices.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana tried to summon her wardlight. The sigil formed, flickered, and folded in on itself until only a faint circle of ash remained.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can't hold it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she whispered.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Even faith has an echo."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan drew his axe, slow and steady.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Forward. Always forward."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Why not back?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Because back isn't there anymore,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, and pointed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The corridor behind them had sealed -- not collapsed, but simply wasn't, replaced by seamless metal still warm to the touch.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik stared.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Topography editing. It's rewriting as we move."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Then we move fast,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed once toward the left passage -- hot, insistent. Not the black-violet of corruption, but the bright warm pulse of recognition. Caelin didn't wait for a vote.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, and moved.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Trusting the thing that just tried to eat us?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked, falling in behind him.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's not trust,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It's recognition."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: As they walked, the walls began to whisper fragments of old conversations: Vex mocking Thornik's kettle, Serana's muttered prayers, Elowen's earlier warning about warmth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain had been listening since Thornwick. Now it was talking back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's hand brushed Caelin's arm.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The flame mark answers to more than one master,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I know,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he replied.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I just don't know which is winning."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The corridor opened into another chamber -- a narrow nave lined with glass coffers filled with ash. Light flickered inside each like dying hearts.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then his own voice came from the darkness ahead:

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I called the fire. I killed her to save you."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Every muscle in Caelin's body locked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That sentence didn't exist outside his memory. No one else had ever heard it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana stepped closer, sword half drawn.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Who said that?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin swallowed.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I did. Once."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The echo stepped forward -- his height, his face, his wound still glowing faintly through translucent skin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It smiled.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Still burning,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: it said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He backed away. The others followed his lead, weapons ready but useless against something made of memory.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The echo tilted its head, movements just slightly off-beat, as if learning how joints worked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When it spoke again, it used all their voices together.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We are the Seal."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum surged; dust fell in bright spirals. The glass coffers flared, each releasing a puff of ash that drifted like breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin reached for fire, stopped himself, forced the heat inward until it was pain and not power. The scale burned angry red under his skin but he held it, contained it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mimic paused -- as if confused by restraint -- and in that instant Serana hurled her dagger through its chest.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The blade passed through light and hit the wall behind with a sharp, clean clink.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The echo shattered into sound -- hundreds of overlapping syllables that faded into silence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When hearing returned, Vex said softly,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Next time, we burn it first."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "No,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We learn why it knows my name."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They left the chamber in silence, the scale in Caelin's arm fading from red back to baseline coal-glow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the shards of light pulled together, reforming a faint outline.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It whispered after them in Caelin's voice, calm and sure:

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Still burning."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They reached the far archway and the space opened like a cathedral of living rock. Three vaulted passages split at precise angles; ribs of stone arced floor to ceiling in calculated load-bearing perfection.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik consulted his journal one last time, marked a route with charcoal.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Junction chambers. The caldera route is our way out."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But the ribs were cracking. Visible stress fractures spider-webbing across supports that had held for millennia. Dust sifted in steady streams. The air smelled of ozone and pulverized granite.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And the sound --

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Deep. Resonant. Felt through boot-soles more than heard.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Seal's heartbeat. Not from one direction. From everywhere. The mountain itself had become its chamber.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Everyone move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, voice tight.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "This entire structure is about to -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain shook.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The junction chamber came apart in stages.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: First: the leftmost rib sheared. Not slowly. Not with warning. Just -- SNAP -- and tons of stone cascaded from the vault in wagon-sized blocks.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "LEFT SIDE!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex shouted as they scrambled right and the floor where they'd been standing vanished under rubble.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain's rhythm broke into a riot: THUD--thud--THUD -- thud--THUD, a crowd of heartbeats not their own. Caelin's pulse tried to follow and stumbled; a sick skip behind his sternum knocked his balance half a step off.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dust hissed from the ribs like breath through teeth. The air carried the wet-clay breath of new fractures and a metal tang, and static prickled along their forearms.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale in Caelin's arm blazed black-violet at the edges -- full corruption bloom, the Seal's influence flooding the chamber like invisible tide. Not close. Not here. But present. Aware. The obsidian plate pulsed irregular, excited: thud-THUD-thud-THUD.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It wants me, he understood. The Seal. It can FEEL the Ember. And it's using whatever power it has to collapse the mountain, to drop me closer to it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Second: the right-hand arch -- their intended exit -- let go like a builder's curse fulfilled. The pre-stressed keystone clicked, a clean crack ran the span, and the arch folded inward in a slate-gray avalanche that sealed the passage throat to floor.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Shadows slid ahead of the collapse -- thin whips testing the raw edge where ward-glyphs didn't quite reach. Caelin's scale flared red-gold; its halo seared those probes to curling soot that skittered away like burned spiders.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Third: the floor cracked. A black lightning zigzag split the chamber from waist to wall and widened with every breath. The Seal's poly-rhythm devolved into pure thrill -- THUD-thud-THUD-thud-THUD -- no pattern, just a chained thing thrashing in joy at the scent of fire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: ELOWEN was at the rear -- counting heads, keeping stragglers from becoming ghosts -- when the slab under her boots lowered, wrong as a breath drawn by stone. She saw Durgan sprint clear; Nyxara took the widening cut in one clean leap; Caelin turned back toward her --

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not enough time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She chose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She slammed her staff and called the new magic: not growth, load-sharing. Roots erupted from living wood, racing across the failing geometry in a glowing web -- but these weren't the sickly pale green of dying nature-magic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They blazed emerald shot through with threads of silver-gold, the Abjuration influence from the Deep Hall now woven into her very being.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The roots didn't brace the mountain; they borrowed weight -- took the load for three breaths, then two, then one -- as the transformed power coursed through her tattoos until the channels screamed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The smell of burning green knifed through dust -- sharp chlorophyll-sweet, life paying itself out, but underneath it the metallic tang of ward-magic being consumed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The web held. The lowering paused.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "GO!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her voice cut the riot clean.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can't hold -- MOVE!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's hand went up -- chalk sigil, instinct, geometry she still believed in -- and the ward simply wasn't there. The mountain swallowed the shape before it formed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She chose the only thing left: she hooked Caelin's harness with both hands and dragged him back before the slab surged. She chose too: one lost or many. Not a prayer. Just arithmetic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He wrenched to go anyway. The floor lurched.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's tattoos burned from emerald-and-silver to pure white. The root-web flared to ash, spent -- not shattered, not explosive, simply gone. The transformation magic that had given her new purpose consumed itself in one final act of protection.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The slab dropped. She went with it, swallowed in a grinding maw of stone. No cry. No light -- neither green nor gold. No staff -- wood splintered as the last charge emptied.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dust boiled and sealed the black mouth into a gray scar.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above, the riot in the rock pitched higher, delighted. Caelin lunged for the edge --

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "MOVE OR -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara started, a warning that became prophecy as their section lowered under their boots.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They fell.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: One heartbeat: solid stone. The next: nothing. Caelin tumbled through choking dust, the world a roll of jagged edges and red sparks. Instinct screamed heat the air -- stupid instinct. Hot wind doesn't hold weight.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He tucked, took the rock on shoulder and thigh, let pain be ballast. A debris slope caught him -- cruel, angled, still better than a pit -- and he shed momentum in skin and blood.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Parallel on the slide, Nyxara fell with the eerie grace of someone who'd made a career out of surviving bad ideas. Pact-power whispered -- no force, no shove -- simply convinced the falling stones she wasn't part of the story.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Angles and experience did the rest. She surfed the avalanche to a jutting ledge and cut speed in a low, bruising crouch.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They hit. Caelin sprawled, gasping; his right arm lit in a lattice of red pain as the scale jarred against bone. The filaments flared angry red, screaming, then slowly faded back toward coal-glow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above, stone sealed on stone.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "CAELIN!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's voice knifed through muffling tons.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "CAN YOU HEAR ME?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He coughed grit.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "ALIVE! WE'RE ALIVE!"

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "CAN'T REACH YOU! ROUTE'S SEALED!"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "WE'LL FIND ANOTHER WAY!"

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "SURFACE EXIT -- EAST FACE -- WE'LL WAIT!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thin as thread, then gone. Either distance, or the mountain decided they'd said enough.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin sat, cataloguing damage: bruises blooming, shoulder shrieking, the lattice aching but holding. He hadn't cast. Could have. Wanted to. Didn't. Small victory.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara tested joints -- grimace, then a shrug.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Sprained. Mobile. Ten out of ten would not recommend."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stood, staggered, found purpose.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Mark the wall. Let them know."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He burned Draconic into a clean slab with a tight ember thread: ALIVE. FOLLOWING HEAT. FIND US SURFACE EAST.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Beneath it, he added Thornik's party sigil -- three short strokes, one long. So the others would know it was them.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's glow dimmed from ember-red back to soft coal as the magic settled.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Somewhere beyond the sealed chokes and ruined ribs, the noise shifted. The riot dropped back to patience; the world's pressure exhaled.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not below, not above -- everywhere. Presence, not location. Influence bleeding through leagues of stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Time to move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, already limping toward the only path that wasn't trying to kill them yet.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Before the mountain changes its mind. Or the pets find a new seam."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stood there a moment longer than the task required.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He kept thinking of small things. The way she'd pressed two fingers to stone and said it listens back. The first time she eased the lattice-burn and he'd been too surprised to say thank you.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The vine-marks on her wrists the morning they left the Deep Hall -- two more blackened, and she hadn't mentioned it, just kept walking. He hadn't mentioned it either. He'd thought there was time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That was the part that would stay with him. Not the sound of the slab. The assumption of time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked once at the sealed scar. No token. No miracle. The stone didn't care, and he said it anyway:

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I'm sorry."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale warmed -- acknowledgment, not comfort.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He put her name away in the place where you put things you can't carry and can't put down. Then he turned into the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Up in the junction they'd abandoned, dust settled where green-and-gold light had been. No voice. No staff. No trace.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain's riot eased back to patient counting, as if the chain below had tested the links and found them holding -- for now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan stood at the edge of the sealed passage, ice-blue eyes tracking the crack where Caelin had disappeared. His shadow had leapt for that crack like a starving animal smelling meat -- reaching, stretching ten feet across rubble before he crushed it flat with will and pain.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The pressure burst something. Blood ran from both nostrils now, not a trickle but a stream. He wiped it with the back of his hand, stared at the red smear, then tucked his hand away.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Faster, he thought. It's happening faster.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one had seen. They were too busy staring at where their companions had fallen.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "We move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, voice hollow but functional.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Find another route. Reach the surface. Meet them at the east exit."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She didn't say if they're alive. The silence did it for her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik unrolled his journal with shaking hands.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Vent shaft route. Steep. Narrow. But it reaches surface."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He marked it with charcoal, the line wavering.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Two hours. Maybe three."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then we climb,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said. Her voice was flat, empty of its usual edge. She pressed a hand to her ribs, felt them creak, and kept it there. She turned to move -- and caught the tail end of Durgan tucking his hand into his sleeve. Not fast enough. The wrong colour on his upper lip.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She held the look for one breath. Filed it. Said nothing. Elowen was gone and Caelin was somewhere below and her ribs were broken and there was only so much room.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They turned toward the vent shaft entrance -- a crack in the chamber wall barely wide enough for shoulders.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the Seal counted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Patient. Waiting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The rubble-slope fell away for what felt like miles. In the dark, distance turned elastic, measured only by the steady coal-glow of Caelin's scale and the pricked red points far below.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He counted steps so he wouldn't count losses. One breath. One more. Again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale sat at coal-glow -- baseline, steady, indifferent to grief. He focused on that. On the temperature of the stone under his boots, the pull of the debris slope, the small calculations of footing. He was good at small calculations.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He had been making them since Thornwick and they had kept him useful and upright and he was going to keep making them now, in this tunnel, in this dark, until the habit of surviving outlasted the habit of feeling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It wasn't working especially well.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara moved beside him with careful economy despite the twist in her ankle, saying nothing for long stretches, then:

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "You're quiet. Brooding, or rationing air?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Thinking,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "About the druid who bought us time."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No softness to it. Just truth.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Yeah."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "She chose,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You honor it by surviving, not by drowning in guilt."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A beat.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "And by keeping that thing in your arm from getting us both killed."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They dropped onto a broader landing. Caelin paused, burned a quick line on the wall: ALIVE ? and beneath it the simple four-stroke sigil Thornik had taught them -- three short, one long.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's glow brightened briefly as he worked, then settled back to baseline coal.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved again. The air warmed by degrees until the tunnel opened like a throat into a chamber so vast it made their footfalls feel impertinent.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Forge Caldera wasn't a room; it was a memory made of stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Tiered galleries ringed a polished obsidian floor. Dozens of cold hearths stood in concentric circles around a central pit, their banks still faintly embered as if the world had simply forgotten to finish cooling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The place smelled of quenched steel and old oil; somewhere in the rock a thin, impossible ringing went on, like the last note of a hammer that never quite died.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin's Ember answered with a low pulse; his scale brightened from coal to ember-red -- not warning, but recognition. Bright warm pulse of resonance.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "This is where they forged the Concord,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara murmured, actual awe loosening her voice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin nodded, chest tight. Heritage felt like grief when it came late.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Across the caldera, a bridge arched over the central pit -- elegant, dwarven, impossible. Beyond it, stairs spiraled toward a pale wash of daylight.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Route's there,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "We skirt the pit."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They set out along the perimeter, keeping the corrupted glow at center-left and plenty of stone between. It wasn't the black-violet ache of the Seal; it was old taint, a bruise that never healed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway around, Nyxara's footing went soft on a talus spill and she started to slide. She caught herself on a carved rail, hissed at the jolt, then whispered something that wasn't words so much as suggestion.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her pact-whisper slid across the debris -- not force but permission -- and the fall obliged, choosing other paths. She righted, breath steadying.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Around them, the scale showed a brief silver shimmer -- proximity to active magic -- then faded back.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Efficient,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Persuasive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she corrected, amused.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "My specialty."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They reached the bridge. On the rail, half-buried in dust, lay a fractured tool: a joint-mark chisel with dwarvish geometry on one cheek and a draconic maker-sigil on the other -- a language of edges and fire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin picked it up, wiped grime with his sleeve, and tucked it into his pack.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Proof,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, thinking of Thornik.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "For the councils who don't believe."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: On the near abutment, he burned the party sigil and a neat ? EAST arrow into the stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "In case they come this way,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara limped onto the span, then paused to breathe.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Not to be indelicate,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, tone going bright with mischief,

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "but we're alone, nearly died, and you still haven't tried to hold my hand for courage. Should I be insulted?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Flirting seems inefficient,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, and meant it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She laughed -- genuine, quick.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Impossible to embarrass. Refreshing."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She patted his shoulder, friendly, not coy.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I'll keep trying anyway. Everyone breaks eventually."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I don't think I'm going to."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "That's what makes it interesting."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crossed without looking down. The staircase beyond breathed knife-cold air flecked with the mineral tang of altitude. Daylight brightened from rumor to promise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed, and the caldera's endless ringing faded behind them like a heartbeat choosing to be memory.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud. Thud. Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Distant. Patient. Still counting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the final landing, a door waited -- dwarf-carved, sealed by ice grown old enough to be stubborn. Caelin set two fingers of heat to the latch: just enough to soften, not enough to cost. The mechanism sighed; the slab rolled aside.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed angry red briefly -- small magic, small cost -- then settled back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Daylight. Sky. A ledge chiseled into the mountain's east face.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air was knife-cold and clean, spiced with sun-warmed lichen and far pine-smoke dragged upslope by a contrary breeze.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara closed her eyes and stood in it like a priest.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Surface,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I had a bet with myself."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "You win?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I always do."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They didn't linger. Caelin oriented -- sun angle, ridge line, memory of shouted directions through stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Forge Road exit should be two hours along this band."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Then let's not give the mountain time to change its mind,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, and they set off along the ledge, the Emberpeaks glowering polite and ancient behind them.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The vent shaft was a stone throat that refused to swallow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik went first, shoulders scraping both walls, muttering cheerfully obscene things at geology. Vex slid after him sideways, breath clipped to keep her ribs from complaining out loud. Serana followed, armor ghosting sparks from old rock.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan brought up the rear, dragging nothing but a long, obedient shadow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Grit rained in fits. Serana laced a paper-thin ward over their faces -- muscle memory, not miracle -- and it skinned the dust from their eyes when the shaft shed a handful of centuries. The light was hers -- steady gold from trained discipline, not divine gift -- and it held.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Remind me,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik grunted, wedging through a pinch,

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "why I volunteered the route most likely to make me a cork."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Because the alternative involved dying beautifully underground,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said. She tried a small breath; her ribs sparked protest. She hid the wince behind a scowl.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "You also said, and I quote, 'Left shaft has the coldest air -- surface that way.'"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Present-Thornik applauds Past-Thornik's olfactory heroism,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, pleased despite himself.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They squeezed into a run of broader tunnel, air colder, cleaner. Frost rimed old soot on the stone. Above them: a ribbon of blue like a promise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana climbed on discipline more than faith. Her goddess had been a static-laced channel for days; the old prayers returned as faint as wishful thinking. Fine. Duty would do. Duty and the ward under her skin, thin as paper and twice as stubborn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind, Durgan stopped once, hand braced to the wall. He wiped his nose with the back of his hand -- checking, confirming -- but the skin came away clean.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not yet, he thought. But soon.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He could feel it building. The intervals between shadow-tests were shortening. What had been hours was now minutes. He dug his nails into his palm until pain returned his hands to him, then climbed on.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At a three-way split, Thornik sniffed the drafts, checked his grandfather's blood-spattered diagram, and pointed left.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Coldest. And it tastes like snow,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, delighted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Your nose is a menace,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, grimacing as she tried to keep pace. The ribs pulled with every breath.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "It's also getting us out,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, gentler than the banter wanted to be.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed another hour, then the shaft widened to a slot of white.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The foursome spilled onto a ledge scraped by old avalanches, the world dropping away in a dignified hurry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sky. Actual, indifferent sky.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik shaded his eyes, traced the east face.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "There -- half-hidden by scree. The primary Forge Road exit. If they made it through the forges, they'll come out there."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "How far?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Two hours along the band. Maybe less if the goats have been generous."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said. She looked once at the mountain, where the wind carved the ridges into saints and knives, and whispered the two words she hadn't let herself say aloud:

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "For Elowen."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand found her dagger hilt, white-knuckled. She said nothing. Didn't need to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They started the traverse, Durgan last, shadow flat and patient at his heels.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Below: Caelin and Nyxara hiked the east-face band, the ledge slanting toward a darker cleft that had to be the exit. Nyxara kept to his left -- habit now, where the drop yawned wider -- and made a show of not leaning on him.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Almost there,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "See? No dramatic rescues required. Very dull."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Would've helped if you'd fallen,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, then realized how it sounded.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "To get it over with."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He grimaced.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "That was poorly phrased."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Adorable,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, delighted.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Points for honesty."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They rounded a buttress; the ledge widened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above: Thornik and Vex took first watch positions overlooking the cave mouth. The air bit their teeth; the lichen patches on sun-facing rock bled a pleasant, warm smell that didn't belong to cold places.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "They're late,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, hand pressed to her ribs.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "They're alive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik countered, because you had to choose which truth to feed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Below: A glint of metal ahead -- a buckle on a pack strap -- then the shape wearing it resolved into Thornik, broader than the mountain remembered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin lifted a hand.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above:

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Movement,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, and Vex was already rising, jaw tight as the ribs reminded her she was made of meat and stubbornness.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana stood slower, as if unwilling to jinx it, and Durgan's eyes found a coal-glow he'd been both dreading and needing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Below: Nyxara waved like they'd come across friends at a festival.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Well. How convenient."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Above:

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "They're alive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, and didn't smile so much as allow the expression to happen to her face.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They met on a rubble-shelf wide enough for six people and too small for grief.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's gauntleted hand settled on Caelin's shoulder -- not a hug, just proof.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You made it."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "So did you,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik checked them both with brisk, professional hands, then blinked when Caelin handed him the fractured chisel. He turned it over, breath catching at the twin maker's marks -- dwarf and dragon, working as one.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "By the old forges,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he whispered.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "This... this is going to shut some very loud people up."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Use it well,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex hung back a half step, letting relief show only in how her knives went away without being asked. She straightened too fast; pain shot through her ribs. She swallowed the sound.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik noticed anyway and shifted his pack off his right shoulder so she wouldn't have to take the wind on her bad side.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's eyes moved across faces, counting.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Not everyone,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, soft for her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one answered. The mountain had already said it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the stone's long throat, distant and patient and impossible to place: thud. Thud. Thud. The Seal's count resumed, indifferent to daylight.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana let the moment last exactly as long as it could bear and not a blink more.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We move. Millbrook is three days. Rest, resupply, information. Then we plan."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "What do we plan?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik asked, though he knew.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "To finish what we started,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "To make it mean something."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They turned down-slope, six where there had been seven, the east wind combing the cliff grass into silver.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Half a switchback below, Vex paused, head canted to the wind.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Don't look now,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said lightly,

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "but we have admirers."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik followed her line. Far down the face, where the scree ran like a river, a single figure crested a rib of stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Too still. Too patient. Even at distance, something about its silhouette felt WRONG -- the way it held itself, the way it didn't shift weight like living things do.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It watched.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then it stepped backward -- not turning, just REVERSING -- and was gone.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Scouts,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, voice flat. Not afraid. Accounting.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Distant,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex confirmed, hand already on a dagger hilt.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "For now."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we take the gift of distance,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, and set them moving again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They left the mountain to its long arithmetic -- counting, waiting, patient as chains -- and followed a narrower math: miles to town, hours to water, steps to sleep.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the Forge Road's cave mouth went back to being rock. The junction chambers settled into their new geometry. And somewhere vast and deep, something chained counted heartbeats that weren't its own.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thud.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the sky was very blue and very cruel and very free.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: END CHAPTER 7

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Word Count: ~18,400 words
